title: Sibsey memorial) Memorial
memorial_type: Sibsey Lancaster Memorial (families funded memorial at crash site)

sources.title: Coningsby Town Council / Sibsey Lancaster Memorial Trust

maintainer.notes: Families funded memorial at crash site.

external_narratives.summary: Heritage record for the Avro Lancaster aircraft crash site and war memorial at Sibsey Northlands. The Lancaster ED503 of 9 Squadron crashed on 29 January 1943 during an engine and airframe test flight, killing all six crew. The aircraft dived into a field for no apparent reason.
key_details: Aircraft dived into field without apparent cause

external_narratives.summary: Sibsey Lancaster Memorial Trust page on the Coningsby Town Council website. The Trust maintains the memorial and holds annual remembrance services for the crew.

Page about the Sibsey Northlands Lancaster Memorial with photographs. The memorial was built and consecrated in 1947, taking the form of a stone cross inscribed with the names of the crew: F/L Robert Lind (pilot), P/O Charles Cocks, Sgt John Doran, Sgt Thomas Henry, and Sgt Donald Brown.

Local newspaper article about the lasting tribute to the Lancaster bomber crew at Sibsey. One crewman was thrown clear of the crash and his remains were collected for burial in his hometown of Dalkeith, Scotland.
key_details: One crewman thrown clear of crash site
Buried in hometown of Dalkeith, Scotland
